Synthesis and Characterization of PLGA-glucose
Lactide (0.684 mol), glycolide (0.838 mol), and D(þ)-glucose (2.2 mmol)
were placed in a flask and heated, while stirring to 1358C in an argon

atmosphere
followed
by
addition of
stannous
octoate
(0.05%). After
10 min, stirring was discontinued and the brown viscous mixture was
reacted further at 130–1408C for 10 h. After cooling 50 mL of methylene
dichloride was added. The mixture was dissolved and the solvent was

separated. The dark brown solution was
treated with 25 mL of
5%
aqueous HCl solution to remove the catalyst. The solution was washed

with water and diluted with methylene dichloride. The
solution was
added dropwise to 500 mL methanol at (cid:1)258C. The product was filtered
and dried at 408C under vacuum.

1H-NMR:
200 MHz;
(cid:1):
5.2
(m, CH lactide),
4.81
(CDCl3),
(m, CH2
glycolide), 1.6 (m, CH3 lactide).
The resulting copolymer displayed a weight average molecular weight
(Mw) of 35,845, number average molecular weight (Mn) of 17,069, and
Mw/Mn ratio of 2.1.
Synthesis and Characterization of PLGA-b-CD
Lactide (0.684 mol), glycolide (0.838 mol), and b-CD (2.2 mmol) were

in a nitrogen atmosphere followed by
heated while stirring to 1408C,
addition of 0.05% of stannous octoate. The temperature was raised to
1708C. After 15 min, the brown viscous mixture reacted further at 1408C
for 10 h. The purification and isolation were carried out similar to that of
PLGA-glucose as described previously.

1H-NMR:
200 MHz;
(cid:1):
5.2
(m, CH lactide),
4.81
(CDCl3),
(m, CH2
glycolide), 1.6 (m, CH3 lactide).
The resulting copolymer showed a weight average molecular weight
(Mw) of 71,345, number average molecular weight (Mn) of 39,756, and
Mw/Mn ratio of 1.8.
